name: French 75

spec.method_detail: Shake gin, lemon, and syrup with ice. Strain into champagne flute. Top with Champagne.

history: Named for the French 75mm field gun used in World War I — the cocktail supposedly hit with the same kick. It was created at the New York Bar in Paris (later Harry's Bar) and popularized in America. The gin version is arguably more historically accurate; the Cognac version is the New Orleans style. Both are correct depending on who you ask.
